refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Validate that all safety alerts and operational policies were honored during measurement window.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe task sequences.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (volume/time, tonnage/time) in real-time and post-operation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle, paired with low-latency teleoperation and real-time safety enforcement, supported by continuous telemetry logging and KPI calculation.
DP-B: Deploy autonomous task planning with human-in-the-loop safety override, combined with vehicle performance optimization and automated productivity tracking.
DP-C: Provide operator-centric task execution via on-site or remote control, with safety services as a passive monitoring/alerting layer, and manual productivity logging.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic cycle, paired with low-latency teleoperation and real-time safety enforcement, supported by continuous telemetry logging and KPI calculation.

potential_sources: Vehicle cycle time optimization is independent of teleoperation latency; both contribute to productivity but can be designed separately.
Safety enforcement (DP) is decoupled from productivity measurement (DP); safety services operate as a constraint layer, not a performance bottleneck.
Telemetry and KPI calculation are passive observers; they do not couple back to vehicle or teleoperation control loops.
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling: vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and measurement subsystems have clear interfaces and can be developed in parallel. DP-B introduces autonomous planning complexity and human-override coupling (higher risk). DP-C relies on manual logging (poor traceability, high operator burden). DP-A is preferred for safety-critical, high-productivity mission.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Low-latency teleoperation network (e.g., 5G, private LTE) is available at worksite. Risk: Network unavailability or degradation could degrade remote operator performance.

Assumption: Vehicle hydraulic cycle can be optimized to meet productivity_target without exceeding safety or durability limits. Risk: Cycle time optimization may conflict with safety constraints (e.g., stability, load limits).
Assumption: Safety services can be implemented as a real-time constraint layer without introducing unacceptable latency. Risk: Safety enforcement logic may introduce delays that reduce productivity.
Assumption: Telemetry infrastructure can reliably capture and transmit productivity metrics. Risk: Data loss or corruption could invalidate KPI measurement.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-A to explicitly specify: (1) target network latency (e.g., <100 ms round-trip), (2) vehicle cycle time target (e.g., 45 sec/cycle), (3) safety constraint enforcement mechanism (e.g., real-time state machine), (4) telemetry sampling rate and KPI calculation method. This will enable clearer DP-to-IM mapping and reduce ambiguity in subsystem design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the end-to-end productivity KPI requirement and is responsible for coordinating task execution, safety enforcement, and telemetry aggregation to achieve the productivity target.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task definitions (dig, load, transport, dump) from Site Manager or Tele-operator. Includes task parameters (location, volume target, priority).

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Transmits real-time and post-operation productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time, safety events) to Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality for monitoring and analysis.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Broadcasts safety alerts and constraint violations to Safety Manager and on-site personnel. Includes alerts from Safety Services subsystem.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity between on-site Vehicle, remote Teleoperation, and cloud/edge Safety Services. Supports low-latency command/response and telemetry streaming.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the productivity KPI requirement. It owns the integration logic, task orchestration, and KPI calculation. Minimal diffusion: only Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns the hydraulic cycle and task execution, but does not own teleoperation, safety enforcement, or KPI calculation. Assigning the full FR to Vehicle would require Vehicle to own remote control logic and safety services, creating high diffusion and poor separation of concerns.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and command transmission, but does not own vehicle control, safety enforcement, or productivity measurement. Assigning the full FR to Teleoperation would require it to own vehicle dynamics and safety logic, creating high diffusion.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and safety monitoring, but does not own vehicle execution, teleoperation, or productivity measurement. Assigning the full FR to Safety Services would require it to own task execution and KPI calculation, creating high diffusion and conflating safety with productivity.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of CR-NGHE-001 because: (1) it is the top-level integration point that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (2) it owns the end-to-end task execution flow and KPI measurement; (3) it has clear interfaces (Tasking in, Telemetry and SafetyAlert out) that align with stakeholder needs; (4) it minimizes diffusion by keeping vehicle control, teleoperation, and safety enforcement as separate, contained subsystems.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it owns too much orchestration logic. Mitigation: Decompose NGHE Solution into Task Orchestrator, KPI Calculator, and Safety Coordinator sub-modules in the next level.

Risk: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces may become bottlenecks if they are not designed for low-latency, high-throughput operation. Mitigation: Specify interface performance requirements (e.g., <50 ms latency, >100 Hz sampling) in DP refinement.

Risk: Network Link dependency creates a single point of failure for the entire solution. Mitigation: Implement fallback modes (e.g., on-site operator takeover, local safety enforcement) if Network Link is unavailable.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly define: (1) sub-module structure (Task Orchestrator, KPI Calculator, Safety Coordinator), (2) internal interfaces between sub-modules, (3) external interface contracts (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) with latency, throughput, and reliability requirements. This will enable clearer allocation of sub-FRs to sub-modules in the next decomposition level.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (integrated vehicle, teleoperation, safety, and telemetry) directly addresses the refined FR (operator-enabled task execution with measurable productivity). The DP specifies a coherent solution approach with low coupling between subsystems. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: NGHE Solution IM may diffuse orchestration logic across multiple sub-modules if not carefully structured. Mitigation: Define clear sub-module boundaries and internal interfaces in next level.
Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces may become diffused if multiple sub-modules attempt to generate alerts independently. Mitigation: Centralize alert generation in Safety Coordinator sub-module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution IM has high containment (95%) and low diffusion (5%). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are contained as separate sub-modules with clear interfaces. Telemetry and SafetyAlert are the only external interfaces, which is appropriate for a top-level integration module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial: measure productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) over agreed period, validate safety compliance, and compare against productivity_target. NGHE Solution IM owns the telemetry and KPI calculation logic needed for verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The refined FR is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution IM, with sub-FRs allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services sub-modules. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and traceable to stakeholder needs.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-A to specify target network latency, vehicle cycle time, safety constraint enforcement mechanism, and telemetry sampling rate.
Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to define sub-module structure and internal interfaces.
Document assumptions and risks related to network availability, cycle time optimization, safety-productivity trade-offs, and telemetry reliability.
Define acceptance criteria for productivity_target (e.g., ≥ 150 m³/h for excavation, ≥ 200 ton/h for loading) and evaluation period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week).

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (vehicle execution, teleoperation, safety enforcement, productivity measurement). The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the NGHE Solution IM is the appropriate top-level owner. However, the requirement is not atomic and cannot be verified or designed at this level. Decomposition into sub-FRs and sub-modules is necessary to enable detailed design and verification.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall enable remote operator to command and monitor vehicle with latency and fidelity sufficient for safe, productive task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The safety services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and prevent unsafe task sequences.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The solution shall measure and report productivity metrics (volume/time, tonnage/time) in real-time and post-operation.

assumptions: Low-latency teleoperation network (e.g., 5G, private LTE) is available at worksite.
Vehicle hydraulic cycle can be optimized to meet productivity_target without exceeding safety or durability limits.
Safety services can be implemented as a real-time constraint layer without introducing unacceptable latency.
Telemetry infrastructure can reliably capture and transmit productivity metrics.
Productivity_target is defined by customer and is achievable with optimized vehicle and teleoperation design.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are documented and can be encoded as safety constraints.
open_risks: Network unavailability or degradation could degrade remote operator performance and reduce productivity.
Cycle time optimization may conflict with safety constraints (e.g., stability, load limits), requiring trade-off analysis.
Safety enforcement logic may introduce delays that reduce productivity, requiring careful latency budgeting.
Data loss or corruption in telemetry could invalidate KPI measurement and acceptance criteria.
NGHE Solution IM may become a 'god module' if orchestration logic is not carefully structured.
Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces may become bottlenecks if not designed for low-latency, high-throughput operation.
evidence_needed: Network latency and reliability specifications for worksite connectivity.
Vehicle cycle time and payload capacity specifications.
Safety constraint definitions and enforcement mechanisms.
Telemetry sampling rate and KPI calculation method.
Representative operations trial plan and acceptance criteria.
